segments.s750-g199: که here begins a subordinate/explanatory clause and is functioning as 'because' (classical usage). ملاح 'mollāḥ' means 'sailor(s), helmsman(s)'. گشت is the past of گشتن 'became/turned', so 'the sailors became ...'. از + کشیدن 'from pulling/hauling' marks the cause: 'from the pulling/hauling'. ستوه is an adjective 'weary, sick of' (result of the hauling). The whole clause: 'because the sailors became weary from the hauling.'
segments.s750-g200: مرا ... بود is an older dative/experiential construction: مرا (to/for me) + بود (there was / it was) — together 'there was for me' or in smoother English 'I had' / 'there were for me'. هامون 'hamūn' is 'the plain' (a region type), followed by و دریا و کوه 'and the sea and the mountain(s)'. Word order places the beneficiary pronoun before بود (classical style); the phrase lists what belonged/was available to the speaker.
segments.s750-g129: بر آنم is an idiom meaning 'I am of the mind / I intend'; بر is a prepositional/particle element attached to آنم. که introduces a subordinate clause 'that'. بینی is 2nd-person singular present/subjunctive form 'you see / you will perceive' used here as a future-orientated perception ('you will see/realize'). پشیمان شوی has شوی (from شدن) as a 2nd-person singular subjunctive/present meaning 'you become' and پشیمان is its predicative adjective 'regretful'; together 'that you will come to be regretful.'
segments.s750-g130: وزین here is actually the fused sequence و + زین ('and from this / and from these'); کرده‌ها is the plural noun 'deeds / things done' (کرده + plural -ها). سوی is a preposition 'toward', درمان is the noun 'remedy / cure', and شوی again is the 2nd-person singular of شدن 'become / turn (to)'. The whole clause means 'and from these deeds you will turn toward a remedy.'
segments.s750-g131: دگر functions as 'further / as for the other (thing)'. آنک (= 'that which') introduces the reported matter 'that which you said'. گفتی here is the reported-speech verb 'you said' (past); z (ز) is the preposition 'about / from / concerning' and زندان و بند lists the things ('prison and fetters/chains'). Overall: 'As for that which you said about prison and bonds.'
segments.s750-g132: گر is the conditional particle 'if'. آمد is the past/virtual 'comes' (here used in a conditional sense). ز ما = 'from us / out of us'. بر کسی = 'upon someone / to anyone'. بر + گزند forms a phrase meaning 'harm befalls' (گزند = 'harm'); together: 'if from us harm comes upon anyone.'
segments.s750-g133: چنین بود = 'thus it was' (چنین = 'thus/so'). تا here is temporal 'so long as / until', and the repeated بود is the past copula 'was'. کار جهان = 'the affairs/work of the world' (کار = 'affair / business', جهان = 'the world'). The whole clause gives a temporal frame: 'Thus it was while the world's affairs existed.'
segments.s750-g134: A simple nominal list in apposition: بزرگان 'the great ones' (magnates), و شاهان 'and the kings', و رای مهان 'and the counsel/opinion of the mēhān (an archaic social group — nobles/chiefs)'. رای is best read as 'counsel / opinion' governing مهان. مهان here is an old social term (not 'guest' in this context).
segments.s750-g135: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. تو ندانی is the negative present/subjunctive sense 'you do not know / you may not know' (ندانی is the present/subjunctive/negative form from دانستن). به موبد بگوی is an instruction 'tell / say to the Mobed' — بگوی is a subjunctive/imperative form of گفتن meaning 'say / tell' addressed to the addressee.
segments.s750-g136: کند here is the 3rd-person singular form of 'to do / to make' (used optative/hortative: 'may he make / he will make'). زین is the fused form of ز + این ('from this / by this'); سخن = 'speech / account'. مر = 'to me' (old enclitic form), تو را is the object 'you' with the object-marker را, and تازه روی is an adjectival phrase 'fresh-faced / renewed in appearance'. The clause says 'May this speech make you appear renewed to me.'
segments.s750-g105: چوآن is an archaic fusion of چو + آن meaning “when/when that.” نامه را is the direct object with را marking definiteness. به من is the recipient (to me). بر + بخواند is the compound verb برخواندن “to read aloud/recite”; بخواند here is a classical narrative form meaning “he read” (the -بر prefix emphasizes the action of reading aloud). Word order is S + (object) + indirect object + verb, typical of Persian with the object fronted.
segments.s750-g106: پر از X is the adjectival phrase “full of X” (پر = “full (of)”), so پرِ از آب‌دیده = “full of آب‌دیده.” همی is an archaic continuous/progressive particle often used before verbs in older Persian. سر فشاند is a compact verbal phrase meaning “bowed/pressed (his) head” (سر = head; فشاند here is used idiomatically for bending/lowering the head). The clause describes simultaneous action/result (he bowed his head, being full of tears).
segments.s750-g107: بدان here is an archaic locution equivalent to به آن / در آن (“in that, in it”); the tokenization in the data splits it but the phrase functions as a single demonstrative/adverbial. نامه is the topic/object “the letter.” در + (بَد) is the clause marker meaning “in it was (said/written)” — بد is an archaic/petrified form related to بود (was) in classical usage. که introduces the reported content; شادان is an adjective “joyful,” and بزی is an archaic verbal form meaning “(thou) shalt live” (here indicating future/optative), so شادان بزی = “you shall live happily.”
segments.s750-g108: که again introduces a subordinate clause (“that ...”). با + تاج زر = “with a golden crown” (با = with; تاج = crown; زر = gold). خسروی is an adjective/noun meaning “royal / kingly.” را is the object/definiteness marker attached to خسروی (topic/object emphasis). سزی is an archaic verb here meaning “to befit/adorn/garland” (the dictionary senses given don’t match this verbal use), so the clause says that one will be adorned/entitled with the kingly golden crown.
segments.s750-g109: که چون is a subordinating combination meaning “for/when, as” (که as conjunction, چون = like/as). ماه آذر is an astronomical/calendar metaphor (Āzar is here a proper name/month or celestial epithet), used like “the moon of Āzar.” بد in classical/persian narrative can be an archaic form related to بود (was) or a poetic filler; here it participates in the temporal/metaphorical framing. و connects to the next temporal phrase روز دی (“the day Dey”) — both items set the auspicious time.
segments.s750-g110: جهان را puts “the world” in focus/as direct object/topic (را marking definiteness/emphasis). تو is the second-person pronoun. باشی is the classical/subjunctive/potential form of بودن here used to express eventuality or future state (“you shall be / you will be”). جهاندار is the noun “sovereign” or “ruler.” کی at the end is interrogative/temporal “when?” (rhetorical), so the clause asks when you will become ruler of the world, using older verb forms and inverted emphasis.
segments.s750-g111: شده + پادشاهی conveys a perfect/resultative idea “has become/was (the) kingship” — here describing the length or state of a reign. پدر is the genitive possessor “father” (father’s kingship). The numeral phrase (سی و هشت) that follows in the line gives the value (thirty‑eight); the clause compactly states that the father’s reign reached thirty‑eight.
segments.s750-g112: ستاره is “star” (often used astrologically/portentously). برین گونه is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning “in this manner/thus” (بر + این گونه compressed to برین گونه). خواهد گذشت is the future construction (خواهد + گذشت) meaning “will pass” or “will transpire.” The clause predicts the star’s passing/astrological event in the manner just described.
segments.s750-g345: کجا is an interrogative/adverbial word here introducing a rhetorical location/setting ('where' / 'in which situation'). سنگ هر مهره‌ای is a noun phrase 'the stone of each bead' (سنگ + genitive-like modifier هر مهره‌ای). بد is an archaic written form of بود ('was') linking the subject to the numeral predicate هزار: literally 'the stone of each bead was a thousand.' Word order is subject + modifier + copula + predicate (numeral).
segments.s750-g346: ز مثقال گنجی is a measure/quantity phrase 'a treasure of mithqals' where ز functions poetically as 'of/from' marking measure. چو is a subordinating conjunction here meaning 'when', and کردم شمار is a verbal phrase 'I did the counting' (کردم past 1sg of کردن + شمار 'count/tally' as the object/complement). The clause therefore reads 'when I made the count (they were) a treasure of mithqals.'
segments.s750-g347: همان 'that/same' points back to the previously mentioned item. در خوشاب is a locative prepositional phrase 'in (the) sweet water/draught' (در + خوشاب). بگزیده صد is an adjective + numeral sequence meaning 'a hundred chosen (ones)' — بگزیده is a past participle/adjectival 'chosen', followed by the numeral صد.
segments.s750-g348: درو is a contractional locative 'در او' meaning 'in it' (in that thing). مرد دانا is a noun phrase 'a wise man' (مرد subject, دانا adjective). ندید is the past tense 'saw' (3rd sg), and ایچ بد is a negative nominal phrase 'nothing bad' (ایچ = nothing, بد = bad). Together it reads 'in it a wise man saw nothing bad' (i.e. a wise observer found no fault).
segments.s750-g349: که introduces a subordinate/explanatory clause ('that' / 'for that'). هر حقه‌ای را marks 'each little casket/pouch' as a definite object (هر + حقه‌ای + object marker را). چو here functions as 'as' or 'when' in a comparative/temporal sense, and پنجه is a figurative noun 'grip/handful' (poetic usage) tied to the following numeral imagery — the clause 'چو پنجه هزار' conveys 'as (if) a handful of thousands' in a poetic way.
segments.s750-g350: بدادی here is the 2nd-person past tense 'you gave' (دادی/دادى in older spelling), with درم as the monetary object 'dirham(s)'. مرد گوهر شمار is a compound apposition/vocative 'man who counts gems' (مرد = man; گوهر شمار = gem‑counter), addressing or describing the addressee. The verb precedes or accompanies its objects in this poetic word order.
segments.s750-g351: صد اسپ = 'a hundred steeds' (صد numeral + classical noun اسپ 'steed'). گرانمایه is a postpositive adjective 'precious/valuable' modifying اسپ. پنجه به زین is a prepositional/relational phrase where پنجه (here 'grip/handful' or a harness part in poetic usage) and به زین 'for/to the saddle' describe the trappings or fittings associated with the horses.
segments.s750-g352: همه کرده is a participial/perfect-like phrase 'having made/prepared all' (کرده = done/made). از آخر 'from the end' = 'in the end/finally', ما is the first‑person plural pronoun 'we', and گزین is the verb 'select/choose' (here poetic/archaic form functioning as 'we chose/selected'). The clause means 'we chose them all at the end.'
segments.s750-g385: کسی کاین جهان داد دیگر دهد is a conditional nominal clause: کسی 'someone', کاین (poetic form of این) 'this/that', جهان 'world'. داد is the past/light perfect sense 'gave/grant(ed)' used here in a hypothetical sense together with دهد 'gives' (دهد is present/subjunctive-ish third person). دیگر دهد 'give to another' or 'grant elsewhere'. The clause as a whole means 'if anyone were to grant this world to another'.
segments.s750-g386: نه بر من سپاسی همی برنهد: نه negates the following; بر من 'toward me' (بر + object), سپاسی 'thanks', همی is the older continuous/emphatic adverb 'still/ever', and برنهد is a classical present/subjunctive form meaning 'places/raises' (literally 'puts upon'). Together: 'he still would not raise any thanks toward me.'
segments.s750-g387: برین پادشاهی کنم آفرین = بر این 'upon/for this' (poetic contraction برین), پادشاهی 'kingship' is the thing praised, کنم is first person singular 'I do/make/say' (here 'I pronounce' or 'I give'), and آفرین is an exclamation/noun 'praise, bravo'. Word order is topical (preposed prepositional phrase) then verb phrase 'کنم آفرین' ('I give praise').
segments.s750-g388: که آباد بادا به دانا زمین: که introduces a wish/explanation 'that', آباد 'prosperous', بادا is the optative particle 'may it be', به + دانا + زمین literally 'for/by the wise land' — here best read as 'may the land (زمین) be prosperous (آباد) by/through the wise (دانا)'. The inverted order آباد بادا is a classical poetic sequence: adjective + optative.
segments.s750-g389: چو یزدان بود یار و فریادرس: چو here functions temporal/conditional 'when' (or 'since/if'), یزدان 'God', بود is the copula 'is/was' (here general/present sense), یار 'friend/ally', و 'and', فریادرس 'deliverer/rescuer'. Classical word order places the copula after the subject: 'When God is a friend and rescuer...'.
segments.s750-g390: نیازد به نفرین ما هیچ‌کس: نیازد here is the verb meaning 'needs' (not 'supplicate' in this context), followed by the prepositional phrase به نفرین ما 'to/for our curse'; هیچ‌کس 'no one' is the logical subject. The whole means 'no one will need our curse.' Note classical order places verb before subject here.
segments.s750-g391: بدان کودک زشت و نادان بگوی is an imperative instruction: بدان is a contracted classical form (ب + دان/آن) functioning like 'to that' or 'tell that one' (poetic / archaic); کودک 'child', زشت 'ugly', و 'and', نادان 'ignorant'. بگوی is the verbal imperative/command 'say/tell' (here 'tell him/that child'). The order is topical/imperative: 'Tell that ugly ignorant child'.
segments.s750-g392: که ما را کنون تیره گشت آبروی: که 'that' introduces the reported content; ما را here is a topical/object-marked 'we/our' (classical ordering: 'ما را آبروی' = 'our reputation'), کنون 'now', تیره 'dark/tarnished', گشت 'became' (past/resultative), آبروی 'reputation/honor'. Literal order: 'that to us now reputation became dark' → 'that our reputation has now become tarnished.'
segments.s750-g201: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. گنج درم‌ها is a genitive/compound-like phrase meaning 'the hoard/treasure of dirhams' (گنج = hoard/treasure, درم‌ها = dirhams). پراگنده شد is a simple past passive/ resultative: 'became scattered' (پراگنده = scattered; شد = 'became'). The clause gives the time/background for what follows.
segments.s750-g202: ز is the poetic preposition 'from'/'by reason of'; دینار نو = 'new dinars' (دینار = dinar/coin; نو = new). بدره is a poetic simile 'like a full moon' and آگنده شد is another past construction 'became filled'. Together the clause says that (the chest/hoard) was filled with new dinars, compared to a full moon.
segments.s750-g203: ز here is the poetic 'from/with' introducing the material/ornamentation. یاقوت and گوهر are coordinate nouns meaning 'rubies' and 'gems'. The token وز in the middle functions as a connective in the line but does not correspond to any provided sense exactly (it stands for a linking element in the poetic phrase). شاهوار is an adjectival epithet 'regal/lofty' modifying the following nouns.
segments.s750-g204: همان is a demonstrative/adjectival intensifier 'those very' / 'the same'. آلت here means 'arms / equipment' (instrument/implements) and جامه is 'garment/robe'; و is the coordinating 'and'. زرنگار is an adjectival compound 'gold-embroidered / brocade' describing جامه. The phrase is nominal (predicate is implied from the prior clause).
segments.s750-g205: چو = 'when'. دیهیم ما is a noun phrase 'our villager / our rustic' (دیهیم = villager/rustic, ما = our/we). بیست و شش ساله گشت is a verbal phrase 'became twenty-six years old' (بیست و شش = 'twenty-six', ساله = '-year-old', گشت = past 'became'). The whole clause gives a time/age reference.
segments.s750-g206: ز is the preposition 'from / by'. هر گوهری = 'every/each jewel' (هر = each, گوهری = jewel). گنجها is the plural 'treasures'. ماله گشت is a past construction meaning 'became (one's) possession' or 'came to be property' (ماله = possession/property; گشت = became). The clause states that the treasures came into possession (contextually: were accumulated/owned).
segments.s750-g207: درم را is the direct object marked by را: 'the dirham' (درم = dirham/coin). یکی میخ نو ساختم is a first-person past clause 'I made one new nail/peg' (یکی = one/a certain, میخ = nail/peg, نو = new, ساختم = I built/made). Word order is object – numeral + noun – adjective – verb, typical for emphatic/poetic expression.
segments.s750-g208: سوی is the preposition 'towards'. شادی = 'joy' and مهتری = 'preeminence / chief place'; و = 'and' links them. آختم is a past 1st-person of an older verb meaning 'I mixed / I joined' (آختم here = 'I mixed/merged/entered into'), so the clause says the speaker moved toward or joined joy and preeminence.
segments.s750-g33: چو + past form شدیم here means “when/once we became”; از + noun (از اندیشه او) marks the source/object of awareness (‘aware of his thought/intent’). آگه is an adjectival/predicative form “aware,” and شدیم is the past plural ‘we became/turned (to be)’. Word order is topical: prepositional phrase (از اندیشه او) + temporal particle (چو) + verb phrase.
segments.s750-g34: از ایران here is literal 'from/out of Iran' (denoting departure or origin). شب تیره is a noun + adjective phrase 'a dark night', بی ره is an adjectival phrase 'without a way/path' (بی + noun), and شدیم is the past plural 'we became / found ourselves' — the clause says what condition they ended up in after the event in the previous clause.
segments.s750-g35: همان راه + جستیم: همان ('that/the same') modifies راه; جستیم is a past tense of the verb جستن used here to mean ‘set out for / sought (a road)’ — in epic style this means ‘we took that road’. The clause is coordinated with the following clause by و and finished with the past verb بگریختیم ‘we fled’.
segments.s750-g36: به دام بلا is a prepositional phrase ‘into the trap/snare of calamity’ (به + noun + qualifying noun). بر + آویختن (here in the negative نِ + آویختیم) forms the verbal phrase بر نیاویختیم; نیاویختیم is the past-tense negative ‘we did not cling/attach/throw ourselves (into)’ so the whole clause states that they did not fall into the trap of misfortune.
segments.s750-g37: از اندیشه او here means 'because of/from his design/intent' (از as causal), placed before the predicate for emphasis. گناهم (گناه + enclitic -م) is the subject/predicate 'my sin/fault', and نبود is the past negative/existential 'was not' — the clause denies that the speaker's guilt derived from that scheme.
segments.s750-g38: جز introduces an exception ('except'). جستن is the infinitive/gerund 'seeking' and is governed by از ('except from/seeking'); از شاه راهم here is a genitival/linked expression meaning roughly 'from the king / toward the king' modifying راهم ('my way/course'); نبود is again 'there was not' — the whole phrase says the only thing (or the only fault) was the act of seeking the king (i.e. seeking the king’s favor); word order is elliptical and typical of older Persian.
segments.s750-g39: شنیدم که introduces a reported clausal content ('I heard that …'). بر شاه من is a prepositional/oblique phrase 'upon my king' (the one affected), بد رسید uses بد (adverb/adjective 'badly/evil') + رسید (past of رسیدن) meaning 'bad befell' or 'evil reached' — a common idiom for 'misfortune befell'. The clause reports news received by the speaker.
segments.s750-g40: ز بردع + verb + چو گوش آن شنید is a temporal sequence: ز بردع 'from/with the cloak' (i.e. cloak as immediate instrument or garment) + verb (here written برفتم — appears in the manuscript as an action done on/with the cloak) + چو 'when' + گوش آن شنید 'that ear heard/when it was heard'. The clause is compressed poetically: 'I (immediately) did something with my cloak when I heard it.' Note: the lemma senses provided for برفتم do not match the verbal meaning in context, so that item is exceptional.
